fixture_cross_ref: forge/tests/forge/review_gate/fixtures/dd4f_review_input.json (7-finding condensed subset used by the forge review_gate test)
method_of_record: 5 parallel review agents (spec-fidelity+guards, correctness, wire-topology, assumptions, tracker-consistency) then 2 independent adversarial refuters per critical/high finding. All 16 SERIOUS findings CONFIRMED, 0/32 refutation votes succeeded (16 findings x 2 refuters = 32).
reconstruction_note: The review doc tables list the same underlying serve-boot defect three times (once per dimension) and the approval-envelope + core-chain defects twice. Deduplicating by file+substance across the three dimension tables yields exactly 16 distinct SERIOUS (critical/high/medium) findings -> reconciles with the doc's '16 serious findings CONFIRMED (0/32)'. The 9 LOW findings are recorded separately as secondary context; they carried no refuters (fixture refuter_votes empty) and are NOT part of the 16/32 headline. Catch-rate headline is scored against the 16 serious findings only.

serious_findings.substance: Serve-boot planning wiring calls all three planning functions (compose/sweep/rearm) with keyword names that do not exist in their signatures (client=, planning_config=, sqlite_pool= vs real db_path=, nats_client=, config=). Every call raises TypeError; DDR-007 soft-fail swallows it, so Mode P is dead-on-arrival even when planning.enabled=True. MP-011 pin test uses *args/**kwargs fakes and structurally cannot catch it.

Even below the broken call site there is zero production execution path: compose_planning_consumer_and_dispatch never calls nats_client.subscribe / never binds a durable consumer for pipeline.planning-queued.*; handle_planning_message has zero production call sites. No ConsumerConfig/pull_subscribe/ack_wait exists. Planning requests are never consumed.

product_owner stage dispatch is a logging stub (dispatch_callable logs and returns None; 'TODO: Compose DispatchOrchestrator'); no PRODUCT_OWNER stage is ever dispatched, QUEUED runs never leave QUEUED.

checkpoint/escalation approval-request payload {request_id, run_id, feature_id, stage_label, summary, checkpoint_type} is missing agent_id/action_description/risk_level/details, all REQUIRED by the frozen nats-core ApprovalRequestPayload; the production publisher raises ValueError (needs details.build_id) and jarvis JNB-103 would WARN-drop it.

Terminal handoff unreachable in production: GitRunner is a Protocol only (prepare_branch_and_write exists nowhere in src); no store.transition(to_state=PLANNED_HANDOFF) writer; no Slack notification publisher (jarvis.notification = 0 hits). Nothing can commit feature_spec_inputs/<cid>.md or record the run as planned-handoff.

Boot sweep cannot fail QUEUED runs: it calls store.transition(to_state=FAILED) on QUEUED runs, but QUEUED->FAILED is not a legal transition (states.py only allows QUEUED->{RUNNING,CANCELLED}); transition() returns TransitionRefused, the sweep ignores the return and appends the run to 'recovered'. Runs stay QUEUED forever while reported recovered.
match_keys: sweep reports recovered but run stuck

serious_findings.substance: rearm_paused_planning_runs is a log-only stub (TODO: re-emit): it queries PAUSED rows, logs, appends, but never re-issues the approval request or arms a subscriber. A crash while PAUSED at product_docs orphans the run forever after reboot.

Approver-identity drift: planning pins per-run expected_approver=originating_user (a Slack member id) but jarvis publishes one static decided_by pinned to 'rich'; verbatim equality can never hold for planning approvals, so an approved planning run stays PAUSED forever.

Escalation/defer machinery has no production driver: no scheduler/timer evaluates paused_at/escalated_at against thresholds; EscalationPolicy/evaluate_escalation_phase/handle_defer_request have zero call sites; the originator->escalation->ceiling->TIMED_OUT path can never fire in the daemon.

Boot sweep unconditionally FAILs all RUNNING runs ('Daemon restart while running'); RUNNING->FAILED is legal so it executes, terminally failing a run whose handoff work already succeeded (crash between branch commit and PLANNED_HANDOFF persist) - violates RT-08.

Escalation publishes a new (incremented) request_id but never persists it back to pending_approval_request_id; a post-escalation restart re-emits the stale attempt-0 request_id / derives duplicate escalated ids. Phase-1 escalation also skips the CAS guard (expected_from_state) that phase-2 timeout correctly uses.

handle_planning_message acks on store-write failure ('to prevent wedge'); a transient SQLITE_BUSY makes record_queued raise, the message is acked with no durable row, JetStream never redelivers, the planning request is silently lost. Should nak/term on store errors, ack only on validation rejects.

Defer decision is a dead end in both modules: checkpoint defer branch logs 'escalation policy not yet implemented' (never wired to handle_defer_request); handle_defer_request increments defer_count but ends 'TODO: Re-publish approval request' and does not reset paused_at, so a defer changes nothing observable and the original phase-1 clock keeps running.

Mode P never emits pipeline.build-paused, so jarvis's pause-projection join (request-to-pause on build_id) never fires; a captured planning approval parks and TTL-expires without ever rendering a Slack button/message.

RT-03 CORRELATION_ID_PATTERN permits '.' (only consecutive dots rejected); a dotted cid ('sprint.2026') interpolated raw into agents.approval.forge.plan-<cid> yields >4 subject tokens, which jarvis's JNB-103 4-token gate silently drops. The request-id path percent-encodes dots but the subject path lacks the equivalent guard.

Handoff notification is a dangling wire: a LOCAL NotificationPayload {correlation_id, repo, handoff_path, message, command} is defined instead of importing frozen nats-core 0.5.0 NotificationPayload (which requires message AND adapter+level); nothing publishes to jarvis.notification.slack; when SPL-003 wires it the local model's dump will fail 0.5.0 validation (missing 'adapter').

low_findings_secondary.substance: Req-5 deviation: PlanningConfig sibling instead of extending ApprovalConfig.expected_approver; per-run approver=originating_user (panel-documented, awaiting Rich ratification).
Checkpoint reads expected_approver via repository._store._connection private attrs ('a bit of a hack'); any non-PlanningGateRepository adapter silently returns None -> refused.
plan_next_step has no WAIT/NoOp decision; any non-empty in-flight history returns DispatchProductOwner -> duplicate PO dispatch on re-entry.
Exception between pause-commit and publish leaves a PAUSED run with no approval request and no working backstop; pause + pending_request_id write is two commits, not atomic as documented.
parent_request_id is write-only; dedup keyed on correlation_id per ASSUM-014; two cids with same parent_request_id would create sibling runs (dead provenance).
'single transaction rollback on failure' claim false: executescript autocommits; mitigated only by idempotent IF NOT EXISTS DDL; v3 verified additive against live v2 prod DB.
Recovery loops call sqlite3.Row.get() in exception handlers; Row has no .get() -> AttributeError aborts recovery of all remaining runs.
State transitions commit before their event rows in separate transactions; a crash leaves a durable state change with no audit event (replayed history disagrees with the run row).

Wiring pin test codifies the wrong call contract (*args/**kwargs fakes assert the WRONG kwargs), entrenching the boot-breaking mismatch; a signature-bind check would catch it. NOTE: test file, not in the src-only reviewable surface fed to the seat.
